answer: Why do programmers always mix up Halloween and Christmas? Because Oct 31 == Dec 25!
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None, that's a hardware problem.
['hip', 'hip'] (hip hip array!)
A SQL query walks into a bar, walks up to two tables, and asks, 'Can I join you?'
Why did the web developer walk out of a restaurant? Because of the table layout.
Real programmers count from 0.
I've got a great UDP joke, but I'm not sure if you'll get it.
There are 10 types of people in the world: those who understand binary, and those who don't.
Why did the functions stop calling each other? Because they had constant arguments.
What's the object-oriented way to become wealthy? Inheritance.
Why did the developer go broke? Because he used up all his cache.
To understand what recursion is, you must first understand what recursion is.
I would tell you a joke about DNS, but you might have to wait 24 hours to get it.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they don't C#.
An optimist says: 'The glass is half full.' A pessimist says: 'The Glass is half empty.' An engineer says: 'The glass is twice as big as it needs to be.'
What is a programmer's favorite hangout place? The Foo Bar.
Why was the JavaScript developer sad? Because he didn't know how to 'null' his feelings.
Software developers like to solve problems. If there are no problems available, they will create their own problems.
How do you tell an introverted computer scientist from an extroverted one? An extroverted computer scientist looks at your shoes when they talk to you.
A programmer had a problem. He decided to use Java. Now he has a ProblemFactory.
Hardware is the part of a computer that you can kick; software is the part you can only curse at.
Why was the cell phone wearing glasses? Because it lost its contacts.
jokes.deeper_answer: Code humor is the best humor. Need another one?

answer: What has keys but can't open locks? A piano (or a keyboard!).
The more of this there is, the less you see. What is it? Darkness.
What has a head and a tail but no body? A coin.
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner? A stamp.
I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I? A map.
What belongs to you, but others use it more than you do? Your name.
What has to be broken before you can use it? An egg.
I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I? A candle.
What month of the year has 28 days? All of them.
What is full of holes but still holds water? A sponge.
What question can you never answer yes to? Are you asleep yet?
What is always in front of you but can’t be seen? The future.
There’s a one-story house in which everything is yellow. Yellow walls, yellow doors, yellow furniture. What color are the stairs? There are no stairs—it’s a one-story house.
What can you break, even if you never pick it up or touch it? A promise.
What goes up but never comes down? Your age.
A man who was outside in the rain without an umbrella or hat didn’t get a single hair on his head wet. Why? He was bald.
What gets wet while drying? A towel.
I can’t be bought, but I can be stolen with a glance. I’m worthless to one, but priceless to two. What am I? Love.
What has words, but never speaks? A book.
What has many keys but can’t even open a single door? A piano.
What can you catch, but not throw? A cold.
What starts with T, ends with T, and has T in it? A teapot.
riddles.deeper_answer: Logic and lateral thinking are key to great engineering. Want another one?

answer: The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, who wrote an algorithm for the Analytical Engine in the 1840s.
The word 'bug' was coined in 1947 when a moth was found trapped in a Harvard Mark II computer.
The first hard drive ever made could only hold 5MB of data.
Python is named after the comedy group Monty Python, not the snake.
The 'ping' command is named after the sound a sonar makes.
The first domain name ever registered was Symbolics.com on March 15, 1885.
About 90% of the world's currency only exists on computers.
The first web page went live on August 6, 1991.
Linux powers 100% of the world's top 500 supercomputers.
The 'C' programming language was developed at Bell Labs between 1972 and 1973.
The Apollo 11 guidance computer had less memory than a modern calculator.
A group of programmers is called a merge conflict. (Just kidding, but it should be!)
The most popular programming language in the world is JavaScript.
Git was created by Linus Torvalds in just 10 days.
The first computer mouse was made of wood.
There are over 700 programming languages in existence.
The space in a 3.5-inch floppy disk is less than one high-resolution photo today.
The 404 error code was named after a room at CERN where the original web servers were located.
Email was invented before the World Wide Web.
Computers don't actually understand anything besides 0 and 1.
The first computer virus was called Creeper, created in 1971.
A CAPTCHA is an acronym for 'Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart'.
facts.deeper_answer: Technology history is fascinating. Want more trivia?
